kpi_sets.description: Covers total sales, order count, revenue by period, refunds, discounts, and tax. Use when the user asks about sales performance, revenue trends, order volumes, or financial summaries.

kpis.name: Total Revenue
kpis.description: Sum of all order totals in the period.

kpis.name: Order Count
kpis.description: Total number of orders placed.

kpis.name: Average Order Value
kpis.description: Average monetary value per order.

kpis.name: Total Tax Collected
kpis.description: Sum of tax amounts across all orders.

kpis.name: Total Discounts Applied
kpis.description: Sum of all coupon/discount deductions.

kpis.name: Refund Amount
kpis.description: Total value of refunded orders.

kpi_sets.base_query: SELECT o.orderId, o.userId, o.orderDate, o.orderTotal, o.orderStatus, o.taxAmount, o.shippingAmount, o.discountAmount, o.currencyCode, u.firstName, u.lastName, u.email, u.userType, c.code AS couponCode, c.discountAmount AS couponDiscount FROM Orders o LEFT JOIN Users u ON o.userId = u.userId LEFT JOIN OrderCoupons oc ON o.orderId = oc.orderId LEFT JOIN CouponsV2 c ON oc.couponId = c.CouponId WHERE o.orderDate BETWEEN :start_date AND :end_date

Orders.table_comment: Core orders table. Each row represents one customer order. Join to Users on userId for customer info.

columns.orderId: Unique identifier for each order. Primary key.
columns.userId: The user who placed this order. Foreign key to Users.userId.
columns.orderDate: Date and time the order was placed. Use for all date-range filters.
columns.orderTotal: Total monetary value of the order in the transaction currency.
columns.orderStatus: Current status of the order (e.g. complete, pending, refunded).
columns.shippingAmount: Shipping fee charged on this order.
columns.discountAmount: Total discount deducted from this order.
columns.currencyCode: ISO currency code for this order (e.g. USD, EUR).

Users.table_comment: All registered users including customers, affiliates, and associates. Join to Orders on userId.

columns.userType: Type of user: customer, affiliate, or associate.

OrderCoupons.table_comment: Maps coupons applied to orders. Join to Orders on orderId and to CouponsV2 on couponId.

CouponsV2.table_comment: Coupon definitions including discount rules and validity periods.

columns.code: The coupon code string entered by the customer.
columns.discountAmount: Fixed discount value of this coupon.

kpi_sets.description: Covers new registrations, active customers, user types, retention, and customer segmentation. Use when the user asks about customer growth, user activity, or account breakdown.

kpis.name: New Registrations
kpis.description: Number of new user accounts created in the period.

kpis.name: Active Customers
kpis.description: Users who placed at least one order in the period.

kpis.name: Customer Type Breakdown
kpis.description: Count of users by type: customer, affiliate, associate.

kpis.name: Repeat Purchase Rate
kpis.description: Percentage of customers who ordered more than once.

kpi_sets.base_query: SELECT u.userId, u.firstName, u.lastName, u.email, u.userType, u.dateCreated, u.countryCode, o.orderId, o.orderDate, o.orderTotal FROM Users u LEFT JOIN Orders o ON u.userId = o.userId WHERE u.dateCreated BETWEEN :start_date AND :end_date OR o.orderDate BETWEEN :start_date AND :end_date

Users.table_comment: All registered users. Join to Orders on userId to get purchase history.

columns.userType: Type of user: customer, affiliate, or associate.
columns.dateCreated: Date the user account was created. Use for new registration filters.
columns.countryCode: ISO country code of the user's registered address.

Orders.table_comment: Orders placed by users. Join to Users on userId.

kpi_sets.description: Covers active subscriptions, autoship order volume, cancellations, and renewal rates. Use when the user asks about recurring orders, subscriptions, or autoship performance.

kpis.name: Active Autoships
kpis.description: Number of currently active autoship subscriptions.

kpis.name: Autoship Order Count
kpis.description: Total orders generated by autoship in the period.

kpis.name: Autoship Revenue
kpis.description: Total revenue from autoship orders.

kpis.name: Cancellation Count
kpis.description: Number of autoship subscriptions cancelled in the period.

kpi_sets.base_query: SELECT a.autoShipId, a.userId, a.status, a.nextProcess, a.dateCreated, a.lastProcess, a.numAttempts, o.orderId, o.orderDate, o.orderTotal, u.firstName, u.lastName, u.email FROM AutoShipPrev a LEFT JOIN Orders o ON a.userId = o.userId AND o.orderDate BETWEEN :start_date AND :end_date LEFT JOIN Users u ON a.userId = u.userId WHERE a.dateCreated BETWEEN :start_date AND :end_date OR a.lastProcess BETWEEN :start_date AND :end_date

AutoShipPrev.table_comment: Autoship subscription records. Each row is one active or historical subscription. Join to Users on userId and to Orders on userId + date range.

columns.autoShipId: Unique autoship subscription identifier. Primary key.
columns.nextProcess: Next scheduled processing date for this autoship.
columns.dateCreated: Date the autoship subscription was created.
columns.lastProcess: Date the autoship was last processed.
columns.numAttempts: Number of payment attempts made for the latest cycle.

Orders.table_comment: Orders table. Used here to link autoship subscriptions to the orders they generate.

Users.table_comment: Registered users. Join to AutoShipPrev on userId for customer details.

kpi_sets.description: Covers commission payouts, PV/GV volumes, MLM rank progression, and compensation plan performance. Use when the user asks about commissions, payouts, downline performance, or volume metrics.

kpis.name: Total Commissions Paid
kpis.description: Sum of all commission payouts in the period.

kpis.name: Personal Volume (PV)
kpis.description: Total personal volume generated by associates.

kpis.name: Group Volume (GV)
kpis.description: Total group volume across the downline.

kpis.name: Active Associates by Rank
kpis.description: Count of associates grouped by compensation rank.

kpi_sets.base_query: SELECT cr.compRunId, cr.userId, cr.commissionAmount, cr.pv, cr.gv, cr.rankId, cr.periodStart, cr.periodEnd, u.firstName, u.lastName, u.email, u.userType, r.rankName FROM CompRuns cr LEFT JOIN Users u ON cr.userId = u.userId LEFT JOIN l_compProgramLevels r ON cr.rankId = r.id WHERE cr.periodStart >= :start_date AND cr.periodEnd <= :end_date

CompRuns.table_comment: Compensation run records. Each row represents one payout calculation for a user in a period. Join to Users on userId and to l_compProgramLevels on rankId.

columns.compRunId: Unique compensation run identifier. Primary key.
columns.commissionAmount: Total commission amount paid to this user in this run.
columns.pv: Personal volume accumulated by this user.
columns.gv: Group volume accumulated by this user's downline.
columns.rankId: Foreign key to l_compProgramLevels.id. Represents the user's rank.
columns.periodStart: Start date of this compensation period.
columns.periodEnd: End date of this compensation period.

Users.table_comment: Registered users. Join to CompRuns on userId.

columns.userType: Type of user: customer, affiliate, or associate.

l_compProgramLevels.table_comment: Lookup table for compensation program ranks and levels.

columns.rankName: Human-readable name of the rank (e.g. Silver, Gold, Diamond).

kpi_sets.description: Covers product sales volume, top-selling products, inventory status, and product revenue breakdown. Use when the user asks about product performance, best sellers, or SKU-level analysis.

kpis.name: Units Sold
kpis.description: Total quantity of products sold in the period.

kpis.name: Revenue by Product
kpis.description: Total revenue broken down by product.

kpis.name: Top Selling Products
kpis.description: Products ranked by units sold or revenue.

kpis.name: Out of Stock Products
kpis.description: Products currently marked as out of stock.

kpi_sets.base_query: SELECT p.productId, p.productName, p.sku, p.price, p.OutOfStock, oi.quantity, oi.unitPrice, oi.lineTotal, o.orderId, o.orderDate, o.orderStatus FROM products p LEFT JOIN OrderItems oi ON p.productId = oi.productId LEFT JOIN Orders o ON oi.orderId = o.orderId WHERE o.orderDate BETWEEN :start_date AND :end_date

products.table_comment: Product catalogue. Each row is one product SKU. Join to OrderItems on productId.

columns.price: Base retail price of the product.
columns.OutOfStock: Boolean flag. 1 = currently out of stock.

OrderItems.table_comment: Line items within each order. Each row is one product in one order. Join to Orders on orderId and to products on productId.

columns.unitPrice: Price per unit at time of purchase.
columns.lineTotal: Total value for this line item (quantity x unitPrice).

Orders.table_comment: Orders table. Join to OrderItems on orderId.

kpi_sets.description: Covers loyalty points earned, redeemed, and outstanding balances. Also includes trip incentive qualification. Use when the user asks about loyalty programs, rewards, or incentive trip progress.

kpis.name: Points Earned
kpis.description: Total loyalty points earned in the period.

kpis.name: Points Redeemed
kpis.description: Total loyalty points redeemed in the period.

kpis.name: Outstanding Points Balance
kpis.description: Net unredeemed points across all users.

kpis.name: Trip Incentive Qualifiers
kpis.description: Number of users who have qualified for the incentive trip.

kpi_sets.base_query: SELECT lp.loyaltyId, lp.userId, lp.pointsEarned, lp.pointsRedeemed, lp.pointsBalance, lp.transactionDate, lp.transactionType, ti.tripId, ti.qualificationStatus, ti.qualificationDate, u.firstName, u.lastName, u.email FROM LoyaltyPoints lp LEFT JOIN TripIncentives ti ON lp.userId = ti.userId LEFT JOIN Users u ON lp.userId = u.userId WHERE lp.transactionDate BETWEEN :start_date AND :end_date

LoyaltyPoints.table_comment: Loyalty point transactions. Each row is one earn or redeem event. Join to Users on userId.

columns.pointsBalance: Running points balance for this user after this transaction.
columns.transactionType: Type of transaction: earn, redeem, adjustment.

TripIncentives.table_comment: Incentive trip qualification records. Join to LoyaltyPoints or Users on userId.

columns.qualificationStatus: Whether the user has qualified: qualified, pending, not_qualified.
